findings.issue: Residual `console.log` on line 122 still present despite round 1 reporting it as fixed. Duplicates the `logger.info` on line 121.
findings.recommendation: Remove line 122 entirely — logger.info already logs the same message.

findings.issue: Duplicated regex constant LIST_PREFIX is identical to the one in extract-tasks-from-text.ts line 227. Same for ACTION_VERBS (line 42 vs line 223). Changes to one will silently diverge from the other.
findings.recommendation: Extract both `LIST_PREFIX` and `ACTION_VERBS` into a shared module (e.g., `src/parser/task-extraction-patterns.ts`) and import from both files.

findings.issue: Round 1 reported this as fixed but `toolCalls?: any` is still present. The type should use `AIChatMessage['toolCalls']`.
findings.recommendation: Replace `any` with the proper type: `{ id: string; name: string; arguments: string }[]` or `AIChatMessage['toolCalls']`.

findings.issue: The LM Studio default base URL `http://localhost:1234/v1` is hardcoded in 8 locations across 3 files (lmstudio.ts, api/ai.ts, ui/api/ai/ai-providers.ts). Changing the default requires updating all 8.
findings.recommendation: Export `DEFAULT_LMSTUDIO_BASE_URL` from the lmstudio adapter or `config/defaults.ts` and import everywhere.

findings.issue: `DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS.includes(color as any)` — `color` is a `string` but `DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS` is `readonly string[]`, so `includes` should accept `string` without casting.
findings.recommendation: Remove the `as any` cast. If TypeScript complains about readonly, use a type assertion on the array: `(DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS as readonly string[]).includes(color)`.

findings.issue: `recognition.onerror = (event: any)` — second `any`-typed event handler in the same file (beyond the `onresult` one flagged in round 1). Both should be addressed together via a `.d.ts` declaration.
findings.recommendation: Create `src/types/web-speech.d.ts` declaring `SpeechRecognition`, `SpeechRecognitionEvent`, and `SpeechRecognitionErrorEvent` to eliminate all three `any` casts in this file.

findings.issue: `(recognition as any).maxAlternatives = 1` — third `any` cast in the same file. A `.d.ts` declaration would fix all three.
findings.recommendation: Include `maxAlternatives` in the `SpeechRecognition` interface declaration.

findings.issue: `this.defaultVoice as any` cast when calling `tts.download()`. The piper-tts-web library likely expects a specific voice ID type.
findings.recommendation: Check the `@mintplex-labs/piper-tts-web` type exports and use the correct voice ID type, or add a local type declaration.

findings.issue: `voiceId: voiceId as any` — another cast to work around piper-tts-web typing.
findings.recommendation: Same fix as above — use the correct type from the library or a `.d.ts` override.
findings.code_snippet: const blob = await tts.predict({ text, voiceId: voiceId as any }, (p) => {

findings.issue: `progress_callback: (event: any)` — the `@huggingface/transformers` pipeline progress event has a known shape (`{ status: string; progress?: number }`) that could be typed.
findings.recommendation: Define an inline type: `(event: { status: string; progress?: number; name?: string }) => void`.

findings.issue: `Record<string, any>` used for dnd-kit drag handle listeners. The `@dnd-kit/sortable` package exports `SyntheticListenerMap` type.
findings.recommendation: Import `SyntheticListenerMap` from `@dnd-kit/core` and use it instead of `Record<string, any>`.

findings.issue: Same `Record<string, any>` for drag handle listeners in `SectionHeader`.
findings.recommendation: Use `SyntheticListenerMap` from `@dnd-kit/core`.

findings.issue: Silent `.catch(() => {})` on `playSoundRaw` — audio playback failures are swallowed with no logging.
findings.recommendation: Add `console.warn` in the catch: `.catch(e => console.warn('Sound playback failed:', e))`.

findings.issue: Silent `.catch(() => {})` on `previewSound` — user gets no feedback if preview fails.
findings.recommendation: Log or surface the error.

findings.issue: Silent `.catch(() => {})` on `voice.speak()` — TTS errors silently swallowed during voice call mode.
findings.recommendation: Log the error: `.catch(e => console.warn('TTS speak failed:', e))`.

findings.issue: `(lastMsg as unknown as Record<string, unknown>).isError` — double cast to access an undeclared `isError` field on a chat message.
findings.recommendation: Add `isError?: boolean` to the chat message type, or use a type guard.

findings.issue: `plugin.instance as any` — deferred in round 1 but noted again because a `PluginInstance` interface with optional methods would be cleaner than blanket `any`.
findings.recommendation: Define a minimal `PluginInstanceMethods` interface with optional `getSettings`, `updateSetting`, etc., and use it in the LoadedPlugin type.

findings.issue: Stale `// TODO: Start UI or CLI based on context` — the comment has been present since early development but main.ts just exports services now. The server and CLI have their own entry points.
findings.recommendation: Remove the stale TODO comment since the architecture has moved past this design.
findings.code_snippet: // TODO: Start UI or CLI based on context

findings.issue: Duplicate code pattern: lines 103-105 and 131-133 both iterate OPFS directory with `(dir as any).keys()` and check `.endsWith(".onnx")`. Only the accumulator differs.
findings.recommendation: Extract a helper `async function listOPFSEntries(dirName: string): AsyncIterable<string>` to reduce duplication.
findings.code_snippet: for await (const name of (dir as any).keys()) {

findings.issue: Project colors are defined twice — once as an array `DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS` and again as a map `PROJECT_COLOR_LABELS`. The label map is not derived from the array, so they could drift.
findings.recommendation: Define colors as an array of `{ hex: string; label: string }` objects and derive both the color array and label map from it.
findings.code_snippet:
export const DEFAULT_PROJECT_COLORS = [...];
export const PROJECT_COLOR_LABELS: Record<string, string> = {...};

findings.issue: Ollama default base URL `http://localhost:11434` is hardcoded in two places (line 24 and line 43). Same pattern as the LM Studio URL duplication.
findings.recommendation: Extract `DEFAULT_OLLAMA_BASE_URL = "http://localhost:11434"` constant.

findings.issue: Silent `.catch(() => {})` on `navigator.clipboard.writeText` — clipboard write can fail in non-secure contexts.
findings.recommendation: Show a toast or log: `.catch(() => console.warn('Clipboard write failed'))`.

findings.issue: Silent `.catch(() => {})` on `reader.cancel()` in a safety timeout. While reader cancel rarely fails, logging would aid debugging stuck streams.
findings.recommendation: Add minimal logging: `.catch(e => console.warn('Reader cancel failed:', e))`.

findings.issue: Silent `.catch(() => {})` on Tauri global shortcut unregister.
findings.recommendation: Log the error for debugging: `.catch(e => console.warn('Failed to unregister shortcut:', e))`.

findings.issue: `private pipelineInstance: any = null` — deferred in round 1, but a minimal interface `{ dispose?: () => void; (input: ArrayBuffer, opts?: object): Promise<{ text: string }> }` would be better than `any`.
findings.recommendation: Define a `WhisperPipeline` type alias and use it instead of `any`.

findings.issue: `throw new Error(...)` used for plugin validation errors instead of `ValidationError` from `core/errors.ts`. Inconsistent with the rest of the codebase.
findings.recommendation: Use `throw new ValidationError(...)` for input validation errors in the plugin loader.
findings.code_snippet: throw new Error(`Plugin "${pluginId}" does not have a default export class`);

findings.issue: `throw new Error('Plugin not found')` should use `NotFoundError` for consistency with core services.
findings.recommendation: Use `throw new NotFoundError('Plugin', pluginId)` to match the pattern in TaskService, TemplateService, etc.

findings.issue: Same pattern — `throw new Error(...)` instead of `ValidationError`.
findings.recommendation: Use `throw new ValidationError(...)` for consistency.
findings.code_snippet: throw new Error(`Cannot remove built-in extension "${pluginId}"`);

findings.issue: `throw new Error('Unknown setting')` — should use `NotFoundError` for consistency.
findings.recommendation: Use `throw new NotFoundError('Setting', `${pluginId}/${settingId}`)` to match the core pattern.

findings.issue: `throw new Error('Command not found')` — should use `NotFoundError`.
findings.recommendation: Use `throw new NotFoundError('Command', id)`.
